actionUnits.condition: MonoBehaviour on this GameObject matches the provided Fire script (class Fire, m_Script guid 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) and uses the exact serialized settings shown (isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, neighbors=[], onNeighborsThreshold=2, mainEffectGo -> ParticleSystem id 1849464941111748095). The object has a BoxCollider (no Rigidbody) so we can only use Trigger if there are parameterless public methods. However, Fire exposes only parameterized public methods (SetIsOn(bool), WaterHit(Vector3), UpdateFireState(bool), FindNeighbors(Fire[], int), NotifyNeighborState(bool)); there are no UnityEvent m_Calls in the scene data to invoke with preset parameters. With neighbors empty and onNeighborsThreshold=2, the fire will not auto-turn-on. Without a water source to invoke IWaterInteractable.WaterHit via collision, we cannot exercise TurnOff logic. Therefore no callable parameterless events exist to manually trigger on this object; additional scene objects or event wiring are required to proceed.
